title: Beam Global Q2 2025 Earnings - Strategic Resilience
text: Beam Global reported second-quarter earnings for 2025 with revenue of $7.1 million, representing a 12% quarter-over-quarter increase but a 52% year-over-year decline. Net loss narrowed to $4.3 million from $4.9 million in Q2 2024. Company achieved 20% gross margin in Q2 2025, with adjusted gross margin of 30% excluding noncash expenses, marking best GAAP gross profit margin in its history. Operating expenses cut by 17% year-over-year to $5.9 million. EV ARC charging units saw 23% quarter-over-quarter increase in orders driven by municipal governments, private sector clients, and environmental organizations. ESS revenue grew 21% year-to-date in 2025 from repeat orders and new Fortune 500 automotive company client. Commercial clients accounted for 60% of year-to-date revenue. International sales rose to 37% of total versus historical 15%. Cash reserves increased to $3.4 million and company remains debt-free with $100 million unused line of credit. $7 million backlog provides visibility into future revenue. Beam Global formed 50/50 joint venture Beam Middle East LLC with Platinum Group UAE and expanded into European markets including Romania, Croatia, and DACH region.
